reasoning: The condition for resolution is two consecutive negative quarters of US GDP growth by the end of Q4 2025, based on the BEA's 'Third Estimate'. Based on actual data published by the BEA through the forecast date of 2026-03-01, there were no negative quarters in 2025. Real GDP increased by 2.3% in Q2 2025, 4.4% in Q3 2025, and the advance estimate for Q4 2025 was +1.4%. Furthermore, Manifold market consensus traded at 0.01 immediately prior to the forecast date. Because two consecutive negative quarters did not occur in late 2024 or 2025, it is virtually impossible to resolve YES. Thus, I estimate the probability at the minimum allowed bound of 2%.
